video.explanation:
Model Explain Ability.  
Most businesses run on trust and being able to open the ML ג€black boxג€ helps build transparency and trust. In heavily regulated industries like healthcare and banking, it is critical to comply with regulations and best practices. One key aspect of this is understanding the relationship between input variables (features) and model output. Knowing both the magnitude and direction of the impact each feature (feature importance) has on the predicted value helps better understand and explain the model. With model explain ability, we enable you to understand feature importance as part of automated ML runs.

video.explanation:
Achieving transparency helps the team to understand the data and algorithms used to train the model, what transformation logic was applied to the data, the final model generated, and its associated assets. This information offers insights about how the model was created, which allows it to be reproduced in a transparent way.  
Incorrect Answers:  
B: Inclusiveness mandates that AI should consider all human races and experiences, and inclusive design practices can help developers to understand and address potential barriers that could unintentionally exclude people.  
Where possible, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and visual recognition technology should be used to empower people with hearing, visual, and other impairments.  
C: Fairness is a core ethical principle that all humans aim to understand and apply. This principle is even more important when AI systems are being developed.  
Key checks and balances need to make sure that the system's decisions don't discriminate or run a gender, race, sexual orientation, or religion bias toward a group or individual.  
D: A data holder is obligated to protect the data in an AI system, and privacy and security are an integral part of this system. Personal needs to be secured, and it should be accessed in a way that doesn't compromise an individual's privacy.

A: R-squared (R2), or Coefficient of determination represents the predictive power of the model as a value between -inf and 1.00. 1.00 means there is a perfect fit, and the fit can be arbitrarily poor so the scores can be negative.  
C: RMS-loss or Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) (also called Root Mean Square Deviation, RMSD), measures the difference between values predicted by a model and the values observed from the environment that is being modeled.  
Incorrect Answers:

D: aucROC or area under the curve (AUC) is used to evaluate a classification model.

What does a good model look like?  
An ROC curve that approaches the top left corner with 100% true positive rate and 0% false positive rate will be the best model. A random model would display as a flat line from the bottom left to the top right corner. Worse than random would dip below the y=x line.

Object detection is similar to tagging, but the API returns the bounding box coordinates for each tag applied. For example, if an image contains a dog, cat and person, the Detect operation will list those objects together with their coordinates in the image.  
Incorrect Answers:  
B: Optical character recognition (OCR) allows you to extract printed or handwritten text from images and documents.  
C: Scene segmentation determines when a scene changes in video based on visual cues. A scene depicts a single event and it's composed by a series of consecutive shots, which are semantically related.

Object detection is similar to tagging, but the API returns the bounding box coordinates (in pixels) for each object found. For example, if an image contains a dog, cat and person, the Detect operation will list those objects together with their coordinates in the image. You can use this functionality to process the relationships between the objects in an image. It also lets you determine whether there are multiple instances of the same tag in an image.  
The Detect API applies tags based on the objects or living things identified in the image. There is currently no formal relationship between the tagging taxonomy and the object detection taxonomy. At a conceptual level, the Detect API only finds objects and living things, while the Tag API can also include contextual terms like "indoor", which can't be localized with bounding boxes.

video.text: For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: You can communicate with a bot by using email.
You can communicate with a bot by using Microsoft Teams.
You can communicate with a bot by using a webchat interface.

video.explanation: Azure Bot Service supports multiple channels including email, Microsoft Teams, and webchat. All three are valid ways to communicate with a bot.

Azure's Computer Vision service gives you access to advanced algorithms that process images and return information based on the visual features you're interested in. Computer Vision can determine whether an image contains adult content, find specific brands or objects, or find human faces.
video.text:
Your company wants to build a recycling machine for cans. The recycling machine must automatically identify cans of the correct shape and reject all other items.
Which type of AI workload should the company use?

video.explanation: Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) is the process of automating the time-consuming, iterative tasks of machine learning model development. It allows data scientists, analysts, and developers to build ML models with high scale, efficiency, and productivity.
video.text: Automated Machine Learning is the process of automating the time consuming iterative tasks of machine learning model development. Select the correct option.
Match the tool to the Azure Machine Learning task.

targets.text: Use a wizard to select configurations for a machine learning run.
Create a Machine learning workspace.
Use a drag-and-drop interface to train and deploy models.

video.explanation: Automated machine learning provides a wizard-based interface to configure and run ML experiments. The Azure Portal is used to create and manage Azure resources including ML workspaces. Machine learning designer offers a drag-and-drop interface to build, train, and deploy models visually.

video.text: You want to create a model to predict sales of ice cream based on historic data that includes daily ice cream sales totals and weather measurements. Which Azure service should you use?
video.explanation: Azure Machine Learning is the appropriate service for training a regression model to predict numeric values (ice cream sales) based on historical data with multiple features (weather measurements, past sales). Language and Azure Bot are not designed for predictive modeling tasks.

video.text: Which service should you use to train an object detection model by using your own images?
video.explanation: Custom Vision allows you to upload your own images and train a custom object detection or image classification model. Computer Vision provides pre-built vision capabilities but does not let you train custom models with your own images. Form Recognizer is designed for extracting data from documents and forms.

video.text: Which of these methods is used to generate additional features?
video.explanation: Feature engineering is the process of using domain knowledge to create or derive new features from existing raw data, thereby enhancing model performance. Feature selection removes irrelevant or redundant features. Model evaluation measures model performance. Model training fits the model to the data.

video.text: Automated machine learning can automatically infer the training data from the use case provided.
video.explanation: This statement is False. Automated machine learning (AutoML) automates the selection of algorithms, hyperparameter tuning, and feature engineering, but it still requires you to explicitly provide the training dataset. It cannot automatically infer or generate training data from a use case description.

video.text: What is the purpose of vector-based embeddings?

options.text: To correct misspellings in the training data.
To represent semantic meaning of text tokens.
To create tokens that include multiple representations of a word in different languages.

video.explanation: Vector-based embeddings map words or text tokens to numerical vectors in a high-dimensional space, capturing their semantic meaning and relationships. Words with similar meanings are placed closer together in this vector space. They are not used for spell correction or multilingual tokenization.

video.text: You are using Azure Machine Learning designer to build a model that will predict automobile prices. Which type of modules should you use to complete the model?

options.text: 1 - Split Data, 2 - Summarize Data, 3 - Linear Regression
1 - Split Data, 2 - Convert to CSV, 3 - K-Means Clustering
1 - Select columns in Dataset, 2 - Split Data, 3 - Linear Regression

video.explanation: The correct pipeline order for predicting automobile prices is: 1 - Select columns in Dataset (select relevant features), 2 - Split Data (split into training/validation sets), 3 - Linear Regression (the regression algorithm). This is then connected to Train Model and Score Model.

video.text:
You have an app that identifies birds in images. The app performs the following tasks:
- Identifies the location of the birds in the image
- Identifies the species of the birds in the image
Which type of computer vision does each task use?
video.explanation: Identifying the location of birds uses Object detection (returns bounding boxes). Identifying the species uses Image classification (assigns a class label to the detected object).

video.text: From the use-cases listed below, select the correct use-case of anomaly detection.

statements.text: Forecasting housing prices based on historical data is an example of anomaly detection.
Identifying suspicious sign-ins by looking for deviations from usual patterns is an example of anomaly detection.
Predicting whether a patient will develop diabetes based on the patient's medical history is an example of anomaly detection.

video.explanation: Forecasting housing prices = regression (predicts numeric values). Identifying suspicious sign-ins by detecting deviations from patterns = anomaly detection. Predicting diabetes from medical history = classification.

video.text: During the process of Machine Learning, when should you review evaluation metrics?

options.text: After you test a model on the validation data.
Before you choose the type of model.

video.explanation: Evaluation metrics are reviewed after the model has been tested on validation data. This is the stage where you assess performance (accuracy, precision, recall, etc.) to determine if the model meets requirements.

video.text: You are designing an AI solution to monitor meetings. The AI model should be able to indicate when people are happy or sad using their facial expressions. Which cognitive service should you use for the solution?
video.explanation: The Face API detects and analyzes human faces in images, including facial expressions for emotions such as happiness or sadness. Computer Vision can detect faces but does not provide emotion analysis.

video.text:
You have been provided the following dataset of employees:

| Income | Postcode | Income Range |
|--------|----------|--------------|
| 25000  | 2000     | Low          |
| 32000  | 2002     | Medium       |
| 95000  | 2000     | High         |

You have to use the dataset to train a model that will predict Income Range of an employee. Which of the following correctly classifies Income and Income Range?
video.explanation: Income is a Feature (input used to make predictions). Income Range is the Label (the value being predicted). Postcode is also a feature but is not asked about here.

video.text: Automated Machine Learning works by running multiple training iterations that are scored and ranked by the metrics you specify. Is this statement true or false?
video.explanation: True. Azure Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) runs multiple pipeline iterations, each trying different algorithms and hyperparameters. Each iteration produces a model that is scored with a metric you specify, and the iterations are ranked by that metric.

video.text: Predicting how many vehicles will travel across a bridge on a given day is an example of what type of machine learning?
video.explanation: Predicting a numeric value (number of vehicles) is a regression task. Classification predicts categories, and clustering groups data by similarity without a predefined label.

video.text: You are setting up a webchat bot that provides responses from a QnA Maker knowledge base. You need to ensure that the bot uses user feedback to improve the relevance of the responses over time. What should you use?
video.explanation: Active learning in QnA Maker uses user feedback (the questions users ask) to continuously improve the knowledge base. It suggests alternative questions based on real usage, which you can accept or reject to refine responses over time.

video.text:
Select the correct options for OpenAI code generation capability.
Note: Each correct selection is worth one point.

options.text: GPT can convert a function from one programming language into another.
GPT can explain SQL queries or tables.
GPT can't write unit tests for our function.
GPT can summarize functions that are already written.
GPT models can help developers code faster.

video.explanation: GPT models can convert code between programming languages (A), explain SQL queries/tables (B), summarize existing functions (D), and help developers code faster (E). Option C is incorrect — GPT CAN write unit tests for functions.

video.text: You are developing a natural language processing solution in Azure for the following use case: The solution should analyze customer reviews and determine how positive or negative each review is. This is an example of which type of natural language processing workload?
video.explanation: Sentiment analysis is the NLP technique used to determine how positive, negative, or neutral a piece of text is. It is the correct choice for analyzing customer reviews to gauge overall sentiment.

video.text: For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: You train a regression model by using unlabeled data.
The classification technique is used to predict sequential numerical data over time.
Grouping items by their common characteristics is an example of clustering.

video.explanation: Regression is supervised learning and requires labeled data. Predicting sequential numerical data over time is a time series (regression) task, not classification. Clustering is an unsupervised technique that groups items by common characteristics.

video.text: According to which Microsoft principle of responsible AI should an AI system not reflect biases from the datasets used to train the systems?
video.explanation: The Fairness principle requires that AI systems treat all people fairly and avoid affecting similarly situated groups of people in different ways. This includes ensuring the system does not reflect biases from training data.

video.text: How are ChatGPT, OpenAI, and Azure OpenAI related?

options.text: Azure OpenAI is Microsoft's version of ChatGPT, a chatbot that uses generative AI models.
ChatGPT and OpenAI are chatbots that generate natural language, code, and images. Azure OpenAI provides access to these two chatbots.
OpenAI is a research company that developed ChatGPT, a chatbot that uses generative AI models. Azure OpenAI provides access to many of OpenAI's AI models.

video.explanation: OpenAI is an AI research company that created ChatGPT (a conversational AI built on generative models). Azure OpenAI is Microsoft's service that provides access to OpenAI's models (GPT-4, DALL-E, etc.) through Azure, not just ChatGPT.

video.text: For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: You can use the Translator service to translate text between languages.
You can use the Translator service to detect the language of a given text.
You can use the Translator service to transcribe audible speech into text.

video.explanation: The Azure Translator service supports text translation between languages and language detection. Transcribing speech to text is handled by the Azure Speech service, not the Translator service.

video.text: You have to extract Total and Subtotal from a receipt. Which of the following services should you use?
video.explanation: Azure Form Recognizer (now Azure AI Document Intelligence) has a pre-built receipt model that can extract structured fields such as Total, Subtotal, tax, merchant name, and date from receipts.

video.text: Training of a custom image classification model can be performed by using the Computer Vision service.
video.explanation: Training custom image classification models requires the Custom Vision service (Azure AI Custom Vision). The Computer Vision service provides pre-built image analysis capabilities but does not support training custom classification models.

video.text: What extracts text from handwritten documents?
video.explanation: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) is the technology used to extract text from images, including printed and handwritten documents. Azure AI Vision includes OCR capabilities for handwritten text.

video.text: For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: A restaurant can use a chatbot to empower customers to make reservations by using a website or an app.
A restaurant can use a chatbot to answer inquiries about business hours from a webpage.
A restaurant can use a chatbot to automate responses to customer reviews on an external website.

video.explanation: Chatbots can handle reservation flows and answer FAQs like business hours on channels the bot is deployed on. However, automating responses to reviews on external websites (e.g., Google Reviews, Yelp) is outside the scope of typical chatbot deployments.

video.text: You have to predict the number of gift cards that will be sold next year. Which type of machine learning should you use?
video.explanation: Regression is used to predict a continuous numerical value, such as the number of gift cards sold. Clustering is unsupervised grouping, and classification predicts discrete categories.

video.text: "Detection of faces in an image" can be performed by using the Computer Vision service. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: The Azure Computer Vision service includes face detection capabilities, allowing it to detect human faces within images.

video.text: You are developing an AI system for self-driving cars. Which of the Microsoft Guiding Principle for AI you should be applying to ensure consistent operation of the system during unexpected circumstances. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Reliability and safety ensures that AI systems operate consistently and safely even in unexpected situations. For safety-critical systems like self-driving cars, this principle is paramount.

video.text: You have an Azure Machine Learning model that predicts product quality. The model has a training dataset that contains 25,000 records. A sample dataset is shown in the following table. Based on above dataset, validate the below statements and, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: Mass (kg) is a feature.
Quality Test is a label.
Temperature (C) is a label

video.explanation: Mass and Temperature are input features used to predict the outcome. Quality Test is the prediction target (label).

video.text: You are training an image classification model, but it achieves less than satisfactory evaluation metrics. How might you improve it?

options.text: Reduce the size of the images used to train the model
Add a new label for "unknown" classes
Add more images to the training set

video.explanation: Adding more training images improves model performance by providing more examples for the model to learn from, leading to better generalization and higher accuracy.

video.text: Your company is planning to develop a web chatbot to provide automated answers to common customer queries. Which business benefit should the company expect as a result of creating the web chatbot solution?

options.text: It will reduce the workload on the customer service agent

video.explanation: A web chatbot automates responses to common queries, directly reducing the volume of requests that human customer service agents need to handle.

video.text: "Recognizing handwritten text" can be performed by using the Computer Vision service. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: The Azure Computer Vision service includes OCR capabilities that can recognize and extract both printed and handwritten text from images.

video.text: Select the correct scenarios of conversational AI workload. Select two correct options.

options.text: Assembly line machinery that autonomously inserts headlamps into cars.
A smart device in the home that responds to questions such as 'What will the weather be like today?'
A website that uses a knowledge base to interactively respond to user's questions.
Monitoring the temperature of machinery to turn on a fan when the temperature reaches a specific threshold

video.explanation: Conversational AI involves natural language interaction. A smart home device answering questions (B) and a website using a knowledge base to respond to users (C) are both conversational AI workloads. Assembly line automation (A) and temperature monitoring (D) are not conversational AI.

video.text: Predicting how many hours of overtime a delivery person will work based on the number of orders received is an example of what type of Machine Learning? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Regression predicts a continuous numeric value. Predicting hours of overtime (a numeric value) based on the number of orders is a regression problem.

video.text: Your organization has an existing frequently asked questions (FAQ) document. You need to create a QnA Maker knowledge base that includes the questions and answers from the FAQ with the least possible effort. What should you do?

options.text: Import a pre-defined chit-chat data source.
Import the existing FAQ document into a new knowledge base.
Create an empty knowledge base, and then manually copy and paste the FAQ entries into it.

video.explanation: QnA Maker can automatically extract question-and-answer pairs from existing documents like FAQ files. Importing the FAQ document directly is the fastest way to populate the knowledge base with minimal effort.

video.text:
What type of AI workload should you use in the following scenario?

Counting the number of animals in an area based on a video feed.
video.explanation: Analyzing video feeds to count animals uses Computer Vision (spatial analysis and object counting).

video.text: "Translating the text in an image between languages" can be performed by using the Computer Vision service. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Computer Vision can recognize and extract text (OCR) but cannot translate it between languages. Translation requires the Azure Translator service.

video.text: You have a solution that analyzes social media posts to extract the mentions of city names discussed most frequently. Which type of natural language processing (NLP) workload does the solution use?
video.explanation: City names are named entities. Entity recognition identifies and classifies named entities such as people, places, and organizations in text.

video.text: Which Azure OpenAI model can transcribe and translate speech to text?
video.explanation: Whisper is the Azure OpenAI model designed specifically for speech transcription and translation to text.

video.text: You need to convert receipts into transactions in a spreadsheet. The spreadsheet must include the date of the transaction, the merchant, the total spent, and any taxes paid. Which Azure AI service should you use?
video.explanation: Form Recognizer includes a prebuilt receipt model that can extract structured data such as merchant name, date, total, and tax from receipt images.

video.text: You are building a knowledge base by using QnA Maker. Which file format can you use to populate the knowledge base?
video.explanation: QnA Maker supports populating the knowledge base from PDF, DOCX, TXT, and TSV files, as well as from URLs.

video.text: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: In a machine learning model, the data that is used as inputs are called [BLANK].
video.explanation: The inputs to a machine learning model are the independent variables (also called features). Labels are the outputs the model predicts.

video.text: Select two correct options for Copilot.

options.text: Microsoft refers "Microsoft Copilot" as first-party copilot.
Microsoft refers "Microsoft Copilot" as third-party copilot.
Microsoft refers "plugins developed by other companies" as first-party copilot.
Microsoft refers "plugins developed by other companies" as third-party copilot.

video.explanation: Microsoft refers to its own products as first-party copilots (e.g., Microsoft Copilot). Plugins and extensions developed by third-party companies are referred to as third-party copilots.

video.text: You are building a machine learning model by using the automated machine learning user interface (UI) and need to ensure that the model meets the Microsoft transparency principle for responsible AI. What should you do?
video.explanation: Enabling 'Explain best model' in AutoML generates model explanations, making the model's decisions interpretable. This directly supports the Microsoft transparency principle for responsible AI.

video.text: You need to return text translated to a specified target language. Which type of natural language processing workload would you use for this requirement?
video.explanation: Translation is the NLP workload that converts text from one language to another. Azure Cognitive Services Translator provides this capability.

video.text: Which type of natural language processing (NLP) entity is used to identify a credit card number? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: A Regex entity uses a regular expression pattern to match structured data like credit card numbers, which follow a consistent numeric format.

video.text: Match the machine learning tasks to the appropriate scenarios.

targets.text: Examining the values of a confusion matrix.
Splitting a date into day, month and year field.
Picking temperature and pressure to train a weather model.

video.explanation: Confusion matrix = model evaluation. Splitting a date into components = feature engineering. Picking relevant input variables = feature selection.

video.text:
In the given scenario, select which type of machine learning should you use.

Scenario: Identify groups of people who have similar purchasing habits. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Clustering groups data points with similar characteristics together, making it ideal for segmenting customers by purchasing habits.

video.text: You have a security system that analyzes images from CCTV to provide authorized staff entry into restricted areas. Which type of computer vision does the system use?
video.explanation: Identifying and authenticating individuals from CCTV footage requires facial detection (finding faces) and facial recognition (matching them to known identities).

video.text: Which of the following workload types matches the following workload: Determining whether a review is positive or negative. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Sentiment Analysis is an NLP technique that determines whether text expresses a positive, negative, or neutral sentiment.

video.text: You are using Azure ML designer to deploy a real-time inference pipeline as a service for others to consume. Where will you deploy the model? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is required for production real-time inference deployments from Azure ML designer.

video.text:
In the given scenario, select the type of AI workload it describes.

Scenario: An AI application that uses images to detect cracks in car windshields and warn drivers when a windshield should be repaired or replaced. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Analyzing images to detect physical defects (cracks in windshields) is a Computer Vision workload.

video.text: "An AI solution that is offered at different prices for different sales territories" is an example of the Microsoft inclusiveness principle for responsible AI. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Offering different prices for different groups is an example of the Fairness principle (avoiding discrimination), not the Inclusiveness principle.

video.text: Which Azure services would you use to create conversational AI solutions? Select two valid options.
video.explanation: Azure Bot Service provides the framework to build and deploy conversational bots. QnA Maker creates a knowledge base that bots can query in natural language.

video.text: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: When building a regression model, labels must have a data type of [BLANK].
video.explanation: Regression models predict continuous numeric values, so the label column must have a numeric data type. Text, datetime, and boolean types are not valid label types for regression.

video.text: You are processing photos of runners in a race and need to read the numbers on the runners' shirts to identify the runners in the photos. Which type of computer vision should you use?
video.explanation: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) reads printed or written text from images. Reading race numbers on runners' shirts is a text-reading task, making OCR the correct choice.

video.text: A company has a team of customer service agents to provide telephone support to customers. The company is developing a webchat bot to provide automated answers to common customer queries. Which business benefit should the company expect as a result of creating the webchat bot solution?

options.text: a reduced workload for the customer service agents

video.explanation: A webchat bot that handles common queries automatically reduces the volume of calls and messages that human customer service agents need to handle, directly lowering their workload.

video.text:
You are using natural language processing to process text from a Microsoft news story. You receive the output shown in the following exhibit.

Which type of natural language processing was performed?
video.explanation: The output shows words and phrases tagged with entity types such as DateTime, PersonType, Location, Organization, and Quantity. This is Named Entity Recognition (NER), which identifies and classifies named entities in text.

video.text: For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: Automated machine learning provides you with the ability to include custom Python scripts in a training pipeline.
Automated Machine Learning implements machine learning solutions without the need for programming experience.
Automated machine learning provides you with the ability to visually connect datasets and modules on an interactive canvas.

video.explanation: AutoML does not support arbitrary custom Python scripts in the training pipeline (that is an ML SDK/Designer feature). AutoML is designed to be accessible without programming experience. Visually connecting datasets on a drag-and-drop canvas is a feature of Azure ML Designer, not AutoML.

video.text: You are building an AI system and need to ensure that the service meets the Microsoft transparency principle for responsible AI. Which task should you include?

options.text: Provide documentation to help developers debug code.
Ensure that all visuals have an associated text that can be read by a screen reader.
Enable autoscaling to ensure that a service scales based on demand.
Ensure that a training dataset is representative of the population.

video.explanation: The Transparency principle requires that AI systems be understandable and explainable. Providing documentation to help developers understand and debug the system directly supports transparency. Option B relates to Inclusiveness, C to Reliability, and D to Fairness.

video.text: Which machine learning technique can be used for anomaly detection?

options.text: A machine learning technique that classifies images based on their contents.
A machine learning technique that analyzes data over time and identifies unusual changes.
A machine learning technique that classifies objects based on user-supplied images.
A machine learning technique that understands written and spoken language.

video.explanation: Anomaly detection analyzes data over time to identify unusual patterns or deviations from expected behavior. It is used for detecting fraud, equipment failures, security breaches, and other outliers. The other options describe image classification (A), Custom Vision (C), and NLP (D).

video.text: What is the potential impact of copilots?

options.text: Copilots can only be used for certain natural language tasks like summarizing text.
Copilots only impact applications used in professional settings.
Copilots can help with first drafts, information synthesis, strategic planning, and much more.

video.explanation: Microsoft Copilots have a broad potential impact across many tasks and settings — including writing first drafts, synthesizing information, strategic planning, coding assistance, and more. They are not limited to specific tasks or professional contexts.

video.text: "A triage bot that prioritizes insurance claims based on injuries" is an example of the Microsoft Reliability and Safety principle for responsible AI. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: A triage bot that prioritizes insurance claims based on injuries is an example of the Fairness principle, not Reliability and Safety. It involves making decisions that affect different people differently, which relates to treating all groups equitably. Reliability and Safety concerns consistent and safe operation of AI systems.

video.text:
You are evaluating a medical research project which uses a large anonymized dataset of brain scan images that are categorised into predefined brain hemorrhage types. You need to use machine learning to support early detection of the different brain hemorrhage types in the images before the images are reviewed by a person.

This is an example of which type of machine learning?
video.explanation: Assigning brain scan images to predefined categories (hemorrhage types) is a classification problem. The model learns to assign each image to one of several known classes. Regression predicts numeric values and clustering groups data without predefined labels.

video.text:
Consider the below scenario:
Deploying an AI service that monitors people of certain ethnicity for closer inspection in a retail store.
The above scenario is a violation of which Microsoft responsible AI principle?
video.explanation: Targeting people based on ethnicity is a violation of the Fairness principle, which requires that AI systems treat all people equitably and do not discriminate based on race, gender, or other protected characteristics.

video.text: What is an example of the Microsoft responsible AI principle of transparency?

options.text: Ensuring that opportunities are allocated equally to all applicants.
Ensuring that the privileged data of users is stored in a secure manner.
Helping users understand the decisions made by an AI system.
Ensuring that developers are accountable for the solutions they create.

video.explanation: Transparency means helping users understand how AI systems work and why they make specific decisions. Explaining outcomes to users is a core example of this principle. Option A is Fairness, B is Privacy and security, and D is Accountability.

video.text:
The following statement follows which Microsoft principle for responsible AI?
"The handling of unusual or missing values provided to an AI system."
Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Properly handling unusual or missing input values is part of the Reliability and Safety principle, which ensures AI systems behave predictably and safely even in edge cases.

video.text: Match the types of AI workloads to the appropriate scenarios.

targets.text: Identify handwritten letters.
Predict the sentiment of a media post.
Identify a fraudulent credit card payment.
Predict next month's sale.

video.explanation: Identifying handwritten letters is a Computer Vision task. Predicting sentiment in text is Natural Language Processing. Detecting fraudulent payments is Anomaly Detection (unusual patterns). Predicting future sales values is a Machine Learning Regression task.

video.text:
You are analyzing images and text from an image. You are using Computer Vision service to analyze images and Text Analytics service to analyze text. You have decided to use only one key and endpoint to access all of your services.
What kind of resource should you create in your Azure subscription?
video.explanation: Cognitive Services is a multi-service resource that provides a single key and endpoint to access multiple Azure AI services, including Computer Vision and Text Analytics. Custom Vision and Computer Vision are single-service resources.

video.text:
Which of the following workload types matches the below workload?
"An automated chat to answer questions about refunds and exchanges on an e-commerce site."
Select the correct option.
video.explanation: An automated chat that answers customer questions is a classic Conversational AI workload. It uses natural language understanding to interpret user intent and provide relevant responses.

video.text:
In the given scenario, select which type of AI service should you use.
"Create a bot from a frequently asked questions (FAQ) document."
Select the correct option.
video.explanation: QnA Maker (now part of Azure AI Language as Custom Question Answering) is designed specifically to create a question-and-answer bot from existing documents such as FAQ pages. LUIS is for intent recognition, not Q&A extraction.

video.text: "Providing an explanation of the outcome of a credit loan application" is an example of the Microsoft transparency principle for responsible AI. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Explaining why a loan application was approved or denied is a direct example of the Transparency principle, which requires that AI systems are understandable and that their decisions can be explained to those affected.

video.text: From the listed scenarios, select the correct scenario where Natural language processing can be used.

options.text: stop a process in a factory when extremely high temperatures are registered.
predict which website visitors will make a transaction.
classify email messages as work-related or personal.
predict the number of future car rentals.

video.explanation: Classifying email messages as work-related or personal is an NLP text classification task. NLP analyzes and interprets written language. Predicting transactions or rentals is regression/ML. Stopping a factory process based on temperature is anomaly detection.

video.text: Match the Azure OpenAI models with their appropriate purpose.

targets.text: models that convert text to numeric vectors for analysis.
models that can generate natural language and code responses based on prompts.
models that generate images based on natural language descriptions.
models that represent the latest generative models for natural language and code.

video.explanation: Embeddings convert text to numeric vectors for analysis (similarity, search). GPT-3.5 generates natural language and code responses. DALL-E generates images from natural language descriptions. GPT-4 represents the latest generative models for natural language and code.

video.text: You are building a service to process images from fashion stores and identify the products of competitors. The solution will use a custom model. Which Azure Cognitive Services service should you use?
video.explanation: Custom Vision allows you to train custom image classification and object detection models using your own labeled data. It is the correct choice when a custom model is required.

video.text: Returning a bounding box that indicates the location of a vehicle in an image is an example of which computer vision capability?
video.explanation: Object detection identifies objects in an image and returns their bounding box coordinates. Image classification categorizes the whole image. Semantic segmentation labels individual pixels. OCR reads text.

video.text: You have a natural language processing (NLP) model that was created by using data obtained without permission. Which Microsoft principle for responsible AI does this breach?
video.explanation: Using data obtained without permission violates the privacy and security principle. Microsoft's responsible AI principles require that data is collected and used with proper consent and protection.

video.text: A traffic monitoring system that collects vehicle registration numbers from CCTV footage is an example of which Computer Vision service?
video.explanation: Reading vehicle registration numbers (license plates) from CCTV images involves extracting text from images, which is text detection (OCR).

video.text: Arrange the stages in correct sequence to plan a responsible generative AI solution.
video.explanation: The correct sequence for planning a responsible generative AI solution is: Identify potential harms → Measure those harms → Mitigate the harms → Operate the solution responsibly.

video.text: Azure OpenAI image generation models can generate images only from prompt text.
video.explanation: Azure OpenAI image generation models (such as DALL-E) can generate images from text prompts, but also from existing images (image editing and variations), so the statement is false.

video.text: You are building an image tagging solution for social media that tags your friends automatically in images. Which Azure Cognitive Services service should you use?
video.explanation: The Face service provides facial detection and recognition capabilities, which is exactly what automatic friend tagging in photos requires.

video.text: Select the correct scenarios of conversational AI workload. Select two correct options.

options.text: A telephone answering service that has a pre-recorded message.
Telephone voice menus to reduce the load on human resources.
A service that creates frequently asked questions (FAQ) documents by crawling public websites.
A chatbot that provides users with the ability to find answers on a website by themselves.

video.explanation: Conversational AI involves interactive, two-way communication. Voice menus (IVR) and chatbots are conversational AI. A pre-recorded message is one-way and not conversational. Creating FAQ documents is knowledge mining.

video.text: An automobile company wants to use historic car sales data to train a machine learning model. The model should predict the price of a pre-owned car based on its make, model, engine size, and mileage. What kind of machine learning model should the dealership use automated machine learning to create?
video.explanation: Predicting a numeric value (car price) based on input features is regression.

video.text: You are building a Language Understanding model for an e-commerce business. You need to ensure that the model detects when utterances are outside the intended scope of the model. What should you do?

options.text: Test the model by using new utterances
Add utterances to the None intent

video.explanation: The None intent in Language Understanding (LUIS) is designed to catch utterances that do not match any defined intent, effectively detecting out-of-scope requests.

video.text: You plan to deploy an Azure Machine Learning model by using the Machine Learning designer. Which actions should you perform in sequence?

options.text:
1. Ingest and prepare dataset.
2. Split data randomly into training and validation data.
3. Train the model.
4. Evaluate model against original dataset.

1. Ingest and prepare dataset.
2. Train the model.
3. Split data randomly into training and validation data.
4. Evaluate model against validation dataset.

1. Ingest and prepare dataset.
2. Split data randomly into training and validation data.
3. Train the model.
4. Evaluate model against validation dataset.

video.explanation: The correct ML designer pipeline sequence is: Ingest and prepare dataset → Split data into training/validation → Train the model → Evaluate against the validation dataset (not the original dataset).

video.text: Assigning classes to images before training a classification model is an example of which of the following feature? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Assigning classes (labels) to images before training is the definition of labeling — it prepares the ground truth that supervised learning algorithms require.

video.text:
You are organising an event that involves posting photos of people wearing sunglasses on social media. You need to ensure that you only share photos that meet the following requirements:

1. Include one or more faces.
2. Contain at least one person wearing sunglasses.

What should you use to analyze the images?

options.text: the Describe Image operation in the Computer Vision service
the Detect operation in the Face service
the Verify operation in the Face service
the Analyze Image operation in the Computer Vision service

video.explanation: The Detect operation in the Face service detects faces in images and returns attributes such as accessories (including sunglasses), making it the right tool for both requirements.

video.text: You need to create a clustering model and evaluate the model by using Azure Machine Learning designer. What should you do?

options.text: Split the original dataset into a dataset for training and a dataset for testing. Use the testing dataset for evaluation.
Use the original dataset for training and evaluation.
Split the original dataset into a dataset for training and a dataset for testing. Use the training dataset for evaluation.
Split the original dataset into a dataset for features and a dataset for labels. Use the features dataset for evaluation.

video.explanation: Best practice is to split data into training and testing sets, train the clustering model on the training set, and then evaluate its performance on the held-out testing set.

video.text:
You are planning to build an application that uses the Speech service to transcribe audio recordings of phone calls into text and then submits the transcribed text to the Text Analytics service to extract key phrases.

You want to manage access and billing for the application services in a single Azure resource.

Which type of Azure resource should you create?
video.explanation: A Cognitive Services multi-service resource provides access to multiple Azure AI services (including Speech and Text Analytics) under a single endpoint, key, and billing account.

video.text: "Generating live captions for a news broadcast" is an example of Speech Synthesis solution. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Generating live captions converts spoken audio into text, which is speech recognition (speech-to-text), not speech synthesis (text-to-speech).

video.text:
For each of the following statement, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.
Note: Each correct selection is worth one point.

statements.text: Monitoring online service reviews for profanities is an example of natural language processing.
Identifying brand logos in an image is an example of natural language processing.
Monitoring public news sites for negative mentions of a product is an example of natural language processing.

video.explanation: Monitoring reviews for profanities and analyzing news for sentiment/mentions are NLP tasks (text understanding). Identifying brand logos in images is computer vision, not NLP.

video.text: "Extracting key phrases from the audio recording of a meeting" is an example of Speech Synthesis solution. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Extracting key phrases from audio involves first converting speech to text (speech recognition) and then applying NLP. Speech synthesis is the reverse process (text-to-speech), so this statement is false.

video.text: Automated Machine Learning enabled you to specify a dataset and will automatically understand which label to predict. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Automated Machine Learning requires you to explicitly specify the target column (label) to predict. It automates model selection and hyperparameter tuning, but the label must be defined by the user.

video.text: In which scenarios can you use speech recognition? Select all correct options.

options.text: Creating an automated public address system for a train station.
An in-car system that reads text messages aloud.
Providing closed captions for recorded or live videos.
Creating a transcript of a telephone call or meeting.

video.explanation: All four scenarios involve speech recognition according to this question source. Closed captions and transcripts are classic recognition use cases; automated PA and in-car message reading may involve a speech pipeline that includes recognition.

video.text:
You are developing a web-based AI solution for a customer support system, which will allow users to interact with a web app that will guide them to the best resource or answer.

Which service should you use?
video.explanation: QnA Maker (now part of Azure AI Language as Custom Question Answering) is designed to build a knowledge base from FAQs and documents, enabling a web app to guide users to the best answer — the ideal service for a customer support bot.

video.text: Select the correct capability of object detection.

options.text: Object detection can identify multiple types of damaged products in an image.
Object detection can identify the location of a damaged product in an image.
Object detection can identify multiple instances of a damaged product in an image.

video.explanation: Object detection can identify multiple types of objects, locate them with bounding boxes, and detect multiple instances of the same object — all in a single image.

video.text:
Using RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) values to identify segments of a customer base is an example of —
Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: Using RFM values to identify segments of a customer base is an example of [BLANK].
video.explanation: RFM analysis groups customers into segments based on behavioral similarities — this is unsupervised learning (clustering), not a classification or regression task.

video.text:
You are processing an image using Computer Vision API, which generated an annotated image shown in the exhibit.

Which type of computer vision was used?
video.explanation: The exhibit shows bounding boxes around individual objects (banana, orange, apple) with confidence scores — this is the output of object detection, which locates and labels multiple objects in an image.

video.text:
Which of the following workload types matches the below workload:
Determining whether a photo contains a person

Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Detecting whether a person is present in a photo is a Computer Vision task (specifically image classification or object detection).

video.text: Match the types of machine learning to the appropriate scenarios.

targets.text: Separate images of polar bears and brown bears.
Determine the location of a bear in a photo.
Determine which pixels in an image are part of a bear.

video.explanation: Image classification labels the whole image (bear type). Object detection finds where the bear is (bounding box). Semantic segmentation identifies which individual pixels belong to the bear.

video.text:
In the given scenario, select the correct principle of Responsible AI.
A predictive app provides audio output for visually impaired users.

Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Providing audio output for visually impaired users is an example of Inclusiveness — designing AI systems that empower everyone and avoid barriers, including those with disabilities.

video.text:
Which of the provided options is considered the calculated probability of a correct image classification?

Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Confidence is the predicted probability score that a classification result is correct. Accuracy measures overall model performance across many predictions.

video.text: You are planning to deploy an Azure Machine Learning model as a service that will be used by client applications. Which three processes should you perform before you deploy the model?
video.explanation: Before deploying a model you must: prepare the data (Data Preparation), train the model (Model Training), and evaluate its performance (Model Evaluation). Data Encryption and Model Retraining are not required pre-deployment steps.

video.text:
For each of the following statement, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.
Note: Each correct selection is worth one point.

statements.text: Azure Bot Service and Azure Cognitive Services can be integrated.
Azure Bot Service engages with customers in a conversational manner.
Azure Bot Service can import frequently asked questions (FAQ) to question and answer sets.

video.explanation: Azure Bot Service integrates natively with Azure Cognitive Services and is designed for conversational interactions with users. However, importing FAQs to create question-and-answer sets is a feature of QnA Maker (Azure AI Language), not of Azure Bot Service itself. Azure Bot Service can connect to QnA Maker, but the import functionality belongs to QnA Maker.

video.text: From Azure Machine Learning designer, to deploy a real-time inference pipeline as a service for others to consume, you must deploy the model to which of the following service? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is the required deployment target for real-time inference pipelines in Azure ML designer when the service needs to be consumed by others in production. Local web service and ACI are for testing; Azure ML Compute is for training.

video.text: You have 100 instructional videos that do NOT contain any audio. Each instructional video has a script. You need to generate a narration audio file for each video based on the script. Which type of workload should you use?
video.explanation: Speech synthesis (text-to-speech) converts written text (the script) into spoken audio. Speech recognition converts audio to text (the opposite direction). Translation converts between languages. Language modeling predicts word sequences.

video.text: What is one action Microsoft takes to support ethical AI practices in Azure OpenAI? Select the correct option.

options.text: Logs users out of Azure OpenAI Studio after a period of inactivity to ensure it's only used by one user.
Provides Transparency Notes that share how technology is built and asks users to consider its implications.
Allows users to build any application, regardless of harmful effects, to ensure fairness.

video.explanation: Microsoft publishes Transparency Notes for Azure AI services to explain how the technology works, its intended uses, and potential harms — helping users make responsible decisions. Option C contradicts responsible AI principles, and option A is a security feature unrelated to ethical AI.

video.text:
For each of the following statement, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.
Note: Each correct selection is worth one point.

statements.text: The Text Analytics service can identify in which language text is written.
The Text Analytics service can detect handwritten signatures in a document.
The Text Analytics service can identify companies and organizations mentioned in a document.

video.explanation: Text Analytics supports language detection and Named Entity Recognition (NER), which identifies entities such as companies and organizations. Detecting handwritten signatures is not a Text Analytics feature — it belongs to Form Recognizer (Document Intelligence).

video.text: Your company is exploring the use of voice recognition technologies in its smart home devices. The company wants to identify any barriers that might unintentionally leave out specific user groups. This is an example of which Microsoft guiding principle for responsible AI?
video.explanation: Inclusiveness means AI systems should empower everyone, including people with disabilities, and should not unintentionally exclude specific user groups. Identifying barriers in voice recognition for different demographics directly reflects this principle.

video.text: You have an AI-based loan approval system. During testing, you discover that the system has a gender bias. Which responsible AI principle does this violate?
video.explanation: Fairness requires that AI systems treat all people equitably and do not discriminate based on characteristics such as gender, race, or age. A gender bias in a loan approval system is a direct violation of the fairness principle.

video.text: You are planning to develop a reservation system for restaurants. Which of the following services would you need? Select two correct options.
video.explanation: Azure Bot Service provides the conversational interface to interact with users. Azure QnA (QnA Maker) enables the bot to answer common restaurant-related questions (e.g. opening hours, location). Together they cover the core needs of a restaurant reservation assistant.

video.text: The first stage in a responsible generative AI process is to identify the potential harms that could affect your planned solution. Select the four steps in this stage.

options.text: Document and share the verified harms
Test and verify the prioritized harms

video.explanation: The four steps in the first stage (identifying harms) of responsible generative AI are: (C) Identify potential harms, (D) Prioritize them by likelihood and severity, (E) Test and verify the prioritized harms, and (A) Document and share the verified harms. Option B (Define) is a distractor overlapping with Identify.

video.text: Which type of machine learning should you use for the following scenario: Predict the sale price of auctioned items. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Predicting a sale price is a numeric output prediction task — that is regression. Classification predicts categories; clustering groups unlabeled data.

video.text: "An automated voice that reads back a credit card number entered into a telephone by using a numeric keypad" is an example of Speech Synthesis solution. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Speech Synthesis (text-to-speech) converts text into spoken audio. Reading back digits entered via keypad is a classic text-to-speech use case.

video.text: You are building a machine learning model to determine a local taxi price at a specific time of a day using historic data from a taxi service database. Which type of machine learning model should you build?
video.explanation: Predicting taxi prices from labeled historic data (input features + known prices) is a supervised regression task.

video.text: Consider the below scenario: Ensuring that the numeric variables in training data are on a similar scale. The above scenario is an example of which of the following category?
video.explanation: Scaling/normalizing numeric features so they share a similar range is a feature engineering step performed before model training.

video.text: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: Computer vision capabilities can be deployed to [BLANK].
options: integrate a facial recognition feature into an app.
develop a text-based chatbot for a website.
suggest automated response to incoming email.
video.correctAnswer: integrate a facial recognition feature into an app.
video.explanation: Facial recognition is a computer vision capability. Text chatbots and email response suggestions are NLP tasks, not computer vision.

video.text: What is an example of a potential task a generative AI application can help solve? Select the correct option.

options.text: Monitoring the temperature in a manufacturing facility.
Collecting real time data and storing it in a database.
Creating a draft for an email.

video.explanation: Generative AI excels at creating original text content such as email drafts. Temperature monitoring and data collection are IoT/data engineering tasks, not generative AI use cases.

video.text: "An AI character in a computer game that speaks audibly to a player" is an example of Speech Synthesis solution. Select the correct option.
video.explanation: An AI character generating spoken dialogue for a player is a speech synthesis (text-to-speech) use case.

video.text: When evaluating the performance of a model, select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: When evaluating the performance of a model, the [BLANK] displays the predicted and actual positives and negatives by using a grid of 0 and 1 values.
video.explanation: A confusion matrix is a grid showing true positives, false positives, true negatives, and false negatives, used to evaluate classification model performance.

video.text: Select the correct Azure OpenAI Service models. Select all that apply.
video.explanation: Azure OpenAI Service offers Embeddings, GPT-4, DALL-E, and GPT-3.5 models. Bard is Google's model and is not available in Azure OpenAI Service.

video.text: Match the principles of responsible AI to appropriate requirements.

targets.text: The system must not discriminate based on gender, race.
Personal data must be visible only to approved entities.
It's important to keep record of the automated decision-making processes, so that authorized individuals can understand the reasoning behind a decision.

video.explanation: Non-discrimination based on gender/race = Fairness. Restricting data access to approved entities = Privacy and security. Recording decision-making processes for explainability = Transparency.

video.text: Cultural denigration is a violation of which Microsoft responsible AI principle? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Cultural denigration violates the Fairness principle, which requires AI systems to treat all people equitably and avoid discriminatory or demeaning treatment based on cultural, ethnic, or other characteristics.

video.text: For each of the following statement, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: For a regression model, labels must be numeric.
For a clustering model, labels must be used.
For a classification model, labels must be numeric.

video.explanation: Regression models predict numeric values so labels must be numeric. Clustering is unsupervised and does not use labels. Classification models predict categories (not necessarily numeric) — labels are class names like 'cat' or 'dog'.

video.text: For each of the following statement, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: The Custom Vision service can be used to detect objects in an image.
The Custom Vision service requires that you provide your own data to train the model.
The Custom Vision service can be used to analyze video files.

video.explanation: Custom Vision supports object detection and image classification, and it is a supervised service requiring user-provided training images. It does not natively analyze video files — Video Indexer is the appropriate service for that.

video.text: You need to track multiple versions of a model that was trained by using Azure Machine Learning. What should you do?
video.explanation: In Azure Machine Learning, you register a model to the model registry to track multiple versions of it. The registry stores each version with metadata such as metrics and tags, enabling version management and deployment.

video.text: You are developing a model to predict events by using classification. You have a confusion matrix for the model scored on test data. Identify how many correctly predicted positives and false negatives there are.
video.explanation: In the confusion matrix, True Positives (correctly predicted positives) = 11, and False Negatives (actual positives predicted as negative) = 1,033.

video.text: What are the tasks that can be performed by using computer vision? Select two correct options.

options.text: Detect the color scheme in an image.

video.explanation: Computer Vision can detect brands and analyze the color scheme of images. Translating text is an NLP task, predicting stock prices is a regression/forecasting task, and extracting key phrases is also an NLP task.

video.text: Which of the following terms would you correctly use for data values that influence the prediction of a model? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Features are the input variables used by a model to make predictions. Labels (also called dependent variables) represent the output the model is trying to predict. Identifiers are unique keys for records, not predictive inputs.

video.text: Which of the listed scenarios is an example of a webchat bot? Select the correct option.

options.text: Translate into English questions entered by customers at a kiosk so that the appropriate person can call the customers back.
Accept questions through email, and then route the email messages to the correct person based on the content of the message.
From a website interface, answer common questions about scheduled events and ticket purchases for a music festival.

video.explanation: A webchat bot interacts with users through a website chat interface in real time. Option C describes exactly this scenario. Option A involves a kiosk translation service and Option B is email routing — neither is a webchat bot.

video.text: An app that analyzes social media posts to identify their tone is an example of which type of natural language processing (NLP) workload? Select the correct option.
video.explanation: Sentiment analysis identifies the emotional tone or opinion expressed in text (positive, negative, neutral). Analyzing social media posts to identify tone is a classic sentiment analysis use case.

video.text: You need to identify street names based on street signs in photographs. Which type of computer vision should you use?
video.explanation: OCR (Optical Character Recognition) is designed to read and extract text from images, such as street signs. Object detection locates objects, image classification categorizes the whole image, and facial recognition identifies people.

video.text: Predicting agricultural yields based on weather conditions and soil quality measurements is an example of which type of machine learning model?
video.explanation: Regression models predict a continuous numeric value (such as yield quantity) based on input features. Classification predicts discrete categories, and clustering groups unlabeled data.

video.text: You are building a tool that will process images from retail stores and identify the products of competitors. The solution must be trained on images provided by your company. Which Azure AI service should you use?
video.explanation: Custom Vision allows you to train image classification and object detection models using your own labeled images, making it ideal for identifying specific products. Document Intelligence handles forms/documents, Face handles facial analysis, and Computer Vision uses pre-built models.

video.text: A smart device that responds to the question "What is the stock price of Contoso. Ltd.?" is an example of which AI workload?
video.explanation: Natural language processing (NLP) enables machines to understand and respond to human language. A smart device interpreting and answering spoken or typed questions is a core NLP use case.

video.text: Which Azure Cognitive Services service can be used to identify documents that contain sensitive information?
video.explanation: Document Intelligence (formerly Form Recognizer) analyzes and extracts information from documents, including identifying sensitive content. Custom Vision is for image tasks, and Conversational Language Understanding handles conversational intents.

video.text: You plan to build a conversational AI solution that can be surfaced in Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Cortana, and Amazon Alexa. Which service should you use?
video.explanation: Azure Bot Service supports multi-channel deployment, allowing bots to be published across Microsoft Teams, Cortana, Amazon Alexa, and many other channels from a single bot codebase.

video.text: Which of the following can be used to build no-code apps that use built-in natural language processing models?
video.explanation: Microsoft Copilot Studio (formerly Power Virtual Agents) is a no-code/low-code platform for building chatbots with built-in NLP capabilities, without requiring coding knowledge. Azure Health Bot is domain-specific, and Bot Framework requires coding.

video.text: You are building a Language Understanding model for an e-commerce business. You need to ensure that the model detects when utterances are outside the intended scope of the model. What should you do?

options.text: Add utterances to the None intent

video.explanation: The None intent acts as a catch-all for utterances that don't belong to any defined intent, signaling that the input is outside the model's scope. Adding diverse out-of-scope examples to None intent helps the model correctly identify irrelevant queries.

video.text: You have a solution that reads manuscripts in different languages and categorizes the manuscripts based on topic. Which types of natural language processing (NLP) workloads does the solution use?
video.explanation: Reading manuscripts in different languages requires translation. Categorizing them by topic uses key phrase extraction to identify the main themes.

video.text: A traffic monitoring system that collects vehicle registration numbers from the CCTV footage is an example of which of the following in the Computer vision service?
video.explanation: Collecting vehicle registration numbers from images is an example of Text extraction (OCR), which reads text from images and video frames.

video.text: You have an app that identifies the coordinates of a product in an image of a supermarket shelf. Which service does the app use?
video.explanation: Identifying the coordinates (location) of an object in an image is object detection. Custom Vision object detection allows training a model to locate and identify specific objects.

video.text: For which two workloads can you use computer vision?

options.text: assigning the color pixels in an image to object names
detecting inconsistencies and anomalies in a stream of data
creating visual representations of numerical data
creating photorealistic images by using three dimensional models
describing the contents of an image

video.explanation: Computer vision can assign color pixels to object names (semantic segmentation) and describe the contents of an image (image captioning). The other options relate to anomaly detection, data visualization, and 3D rendering respectively.

video.text: You need to provide customers with the ability to query the status of orders by using phones, social media, or digital assistants. What should you use?
video.explanation: Azure Bot Service enables building conversational bots that can be deployed across multiple channels including phone, social media, and digital assistants.

video.text: What do you call the data that is used as input in a machine learning model?
video.explanation: Features are the input variables used by a machine learning model to make predictions. Labels are the output/target variable the model learns to predict.

video.text: Match the types of computer vision workloads to the appropriate scenarios. Each workload type may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

targets.text: Generate captions for Images.
Extract movie title names from movie poster images.
Locate Vehicle in images.

video.explanation: Image classification assigns labels to images (generating captions). OCR extracts text from images (movie title names). Object detection locates objects within images (vehicles).

video.text: Which AI service can you use to extract intent from a user input such as "Call me back later"?
video.explanation: The Azure Language service includes Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), which is designed to extract intent and entities from natural language user inputs.

video.text: Which of the following is an example of detecting unusual temperature fluctuations for a large machine?
video.explanation: Detecting unusual temperature fluctuations is anomaly detection — identifying deviations from expected patterns in sensor or time-series data.

video.text: You need to convert receipts into transactions in a spreadsheet. The spreadsheet must include the date of the transaction, the merchant, the total spent, and any taxes paid. Which Azure AI service should you use?
video.explanation: Azure AI Document Intelligence (formerly Form Recognizer) can extract structured data from receipts, including date, merchant, total, and tax fields.

video.text: Which of the following displays the predicted and actual positives and negatives by using a grid of 0 and 1 values when evaluating the performance of a model?
video.explanation: A confusion matrix shows predicted vs. actual classification results in a grid format using true/false positives and negatives (0s and 1s).

video.text: Which type of natural language processing (NLP) entity is used to identify a phone number?
video.explanation: Regular expression entities use defined patterns to match structured data like phone numbers, email addresses, or postal codes.

video.text: You are developing a solution that uses the Language service. You need to identify the main talking points in a collection of documents. Which type of natural language processing should you use?
video.explanation: Key phrase extraction identifies the main topics and talking points within text documents.

video.text: For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: A bot that responds to queries by internal users is an example of a natural language processing model.
A mobile application that displays images relating to an entered search term is an example of a natural language processing workload.
A web form used to submit a request to reset a password is an example of a natural language processing workload.

video.explanation: A bot uses NLP to understand user queries (Yes). Displaying images from a search term is a computer vision/search workload, not NLP (No). A password reset form is a basic web form with no NLP involved (No).

video.text: You have a website that includes customer reviews. You need to store the reviews in English and present the reviews to users in their respective language by recognizing each user's geographical location. Which type of natural language processing workload should you use?
video.explanation: Translation converts text from one language to another, enabling the reviews stored in English to be presented to users in their own language.

video.text: Match the machine learning models to the appropriate description. Each model type may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

targets.text: A supervised machine learning model used to predict numeric values.
A supervised machine learning model used to predict categories.
An unsupervised machine learning model used to group similar entities based on features.

video.text: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: The interactive answering of questions entered by a user as part of an application is an example of [BLANK].

video.text:
You are building a chatbot that will use natural language processing (NLP) to perform the following actions based on the text input of a user:
• Accept customer orders.
• Retrieve support documents.
• Retrieve order status updates.
Which type of NLP should you use?
You plan to develop a bot that will enable users to query a knowledge base by using natural language processing. Which two services should you include in the solution?
Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. Natural language processing can be used to ___.

options.text: Classify email messages as work-related or personal.

video.text: You need to develop a mobile app for employees to scan and store their expenses while travelling. Which type of computer vision should you use?
Your company manufactures widgets. You have 1,000 digital photos of the widgets. You need to identify the location of the widgets within the photos. What should you use?
For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: A smart device in the home that responds to questions such as "when is my next appointment?" is an example of conversational AI.
An interactive webchat feature on a company website can be implemented by using Azure Bot Service.
Automatically generating captions for pre-recorded videos is an example of conversational AI.

video.text: You need to implement a pre-built solution that will identify well-known brands in digital photographs. Which Azure Cognitive Services service should you use?
Match the Azure Cognitive Services to the appropriate actions. Each service may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

targets.text: Identify objects in an Image.
Automatically import data from an invoice to a database.
Identify people in an image.

video.text: Which Azure service can you use as a security information and event management (SIEM) solution?
For each of the following statements, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

statements.text: Organizing documents into groups based on different usage statistics is an example of clustering.
Grouping similar patients based on symptoms and diagnostics test results is an example of clustering.
Predicting whether a person will develop mild, moderate, or severe allergy symptoms based on pollen count is an example of clustering.

video.text: You plan to deploy an Azure Machine Learning model by using the Machine Learning designer. Which four actions should you perform in sequence?
items: B. Split the data randomly into training data and validation data.
C. Evaluate the model against the original dataset.
D. Evaluate the model against the validation dataset.
E. Ingest and prepare a dataset.

targets.text: Step 1
targets.correctItem: E. Ingest and prepare a dataset.

targets.text: Step 2
targets.correctItem: B. Split the data randomly into training data and validation data.

targets.text: Step 3
Step 4
targets.correctItem: D. Evaluate the model against the validation dataset.

video.text: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.
video.sentence: In a machine learning model, the data that is used as inputs are called [BLANK].

video.text: You need to create a customer support solution to help customers access information. The solution must support email, phone, and live chat channels. Which type of AI solution should you use?
